entities.name: Model inference errors
entities.evidence: unions of Response subclasses have historically led to model inference errors unless response_model=None was explicitly set

entities.name: PR 15040
entities.evidence: This PR aims to address those inconsistencies while preserving FastAPI’s runtime design principles.

entities.name: Mixed unions
entities.evidence: Mixed unions combining Response subclasses with Pydantic models or primitive types.

entities.name: Response subclasses in return annotations
entities.evidence: Response subclasses found in return annotations are extracted before response model inference.

relationships.evidence: Unions of Response subclasses no longer raise model inference errors, and explicit response_model=None is no longer required in those scenarios.

Response subclasses found in return annotations are extracted before response model inference. They are excluded from Pydantic model field creation and instead used exclusively for OpenAPI documentation.
I refined the original idea to better align with FastAPI’s design principles.
They are excluded from Pydantic model field creation and instead used exclusively for OpenAPI documentation.
The generated OpenAPI schema now reflects: Media types of Response subclasses declared in return annotations.
The generated OpenAPI schema now reflects: ... Mixed unions combining Response subclasses with Pydantic models or primitive types.

relationships.evidence: Bumps strawberry-graphql from 0.291.2 to 0.307.1.
Private fields are auto-populated from pydantic models: When a strawberry.Private field has the same name as a pydantic model field, from_pydantic() will automatically populate it from the model
Mark fields as strawberry.Private to hide them from the GraphQL schema
Previously, using all_fields=True would override any explicitly defined fields in the strawberry type. Now, explicit definitions take precedence
Source ID: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15033

entities.name: Direct downloads
entities.evidence: Direct downloads. To support direct uploads in actions/upload-artifact, the action will no longer attempt to unzip all downloaded files.

entities.name: Enforced checks
entities.evidence: Enforced checks (breaking). A previous release introduced digest checks on the download.

entities.name: ESM
entities.evidence: To support new versions of the @actions/* packages, we've upgraded the package to ESM.

entities.name: Dependabot
entities.evidence: Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself.

entities.name: skip-decompress
entities.evidence: Callers wishing to download a zipped file as-is can also set the new skip-decompress parameter to false.

entities.name: digest-mismatch
entities.evidence: Callers can now configure the behavior on mismatch with the digest-mismatch parameter.

relationships.evidence: Bumps actions/download-artifact from 7 to 8.

we've upgraded the package to ESM.
Callers wishing to download a zipped file as-is can also set the new skip-decompress parameter
Callers can now configure the behavior on mismatch with the digest-mismatch parameter.

used with app-factory patterns

entities.evidence: https://github.com/jvllmr/fastapi-deferred-init
In my FastAPI project I have a lot of nested routers.
I soon reached a FastAPI start-up time of 30 seconds or more.
Defer the calculation of values until they are actually needed.
introducing an argument to the APIRouter
and APIRoute which decides if values are deferred or not

relationships.evidence: This pull request is my proposal to integrate this performance optimization into FastAPI
By doing that the start-up time was shortened significantly.
Because the amount of nested routers I soon reached a FastAPI start-up time of 30 seconds or more.
introducing an argument to the APIRouter ... which decides if values are deferred or not
introducing an argument to the ... APIRoute which decides if values are deferred or not
I soon reached a FastAPI start-up time of 30 seconds or more. This hurt my developer experience a lot and I soon began digging in to why FastAPI takes so long to load.
